candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary below EBITDA, but not labelled as COGS/cost of sales and no COGS-specific D&A row is shown.
Component of the total D&A block, but not identified as COGS-specific; summary row exists so component should not be preferred.
Minor component/adjustment within the D&A block, not COGS-specific and should not be selected over the summary row.
Balancing line rather than a substantive COGS D&A expense row.

variables.notes: No explicit depreciation/amortisation row tied to Cost of Sales or cost of revenues is visible. The only D&A rows are operating-level summary/components below EBITDA, so adding any of them to COGS would risk misallocation and double counting.

candidates.why: Explicit separate operating D&A summary line below EBITDA and above EBIT, appearing outside the selected SG&A component rows; if any D&A is to be added separately to overhead, this is the preferred summary row.
Component within the D&A block, but should not be selected because the summary row exists.
Adjustment/component within the D&A block, not preferred when the summary row is available.
Balancing line rather than a substantive add-back row.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA bucket consists of personnel and external-expense components only, and the statement separately presents a standalone operating D&A summary below EBITDA. Per the same-level separate-row rule, this D&A appears outside those selected SG&A components and should be added separately. Summary row chosen over components to avoid double counting.
